pr.title: Label some common functions to help better identify possible splits
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly/header-only symbol labeling PR. It replaced many anonymous func_*/lbl_* names with subsystem-prefixed names across fighter, effects, item, player, menu, and fighter callback tables, primarily to make common routines and table-driven boundaries easier to recognize when planning future splits. The diff is almost entirely one-for-one label/callsite/comment renaming with balanced additions/deletions, not behavioral code changes.
what_changed: Renamed the fighter callback table at lbl_803C1154 to ft_OnLoad and its entries from raw func_* labels to per-character OnLoad labels such as ftMario_OnLoad, ftFox_OnLoad, ftCFalcon_OnLoad, ftDonkey_OnLoad, ftKirby_OnLoad, ftKoopa_OnLoad, ftLink_OnLoad, ftSeak_OnLoad, ftNess_OnLoad, ftPeach_OnLoad, ftPopo_OnLoad, ftNana_OnLoad, ftPikachu_OnLoad, ftSamus_OnLoad, ftYoshi_OnLoad, ftPurin_OnLoad, ftMewtwo_OnLoad, ftLuigi_OnLoad, ftMars_OnLoad, ftZelda_OnLoad, ftCLink_OnLoad, ftDrMario_OnLoad, ftFalco_OnLoad, ftPichu_OnLoad, ftGameWatch_OnLoad, ftGanon_OnLoad, ftRoy_OnLoad, ftMasterhand_OnLoad, ftCrazyhand_OnLoad, ftZakoBoy_OnLoad, ftZakoGirl_OnLoad, ftGKoopa_OnLoad, and ftSandbag_OnLoad.

Labeled multiple fighter action/callback tables in asm/data.s and include/variables.h, including ft_OnDeath, ft_SpecialS, ft_SpecialHiAir, ft_SpecialLwAir, ft_SpecialSAir, ft_SpecialNAir, ft_SpecialN, ft_SpecialLw, ft_SpecialHi, ft_OnAbsorb, ft_OnItemPickup, ft_OnItemInvisible, ft_OnItemVisible, ft_OnItemRelease, ft_OnItemCatch, and ft_OnKnockbackEnter.

Renamed common effects-library routines in asm/melee/ef/code_8005B4B8.s and propagated callsites: func_8005B880 became efLib_DestroyAll, func_8005BA40 became efLib_PauseAll, func_8005BAC4 became efLib_ResumeAll, func_8005FDDC became ef_Spawn, and func_800676F0 became efAsync_Spawn.

Renamed common fighter animation/data routines in asm/melee/ft/code_8006DBF4.s, including func_8006F190 to ftAnim_SetAnimRate, func_80070B88 to ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim, func_8007D4F4 to ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed, and func_800866A4 to ftData_SetScale, with branch/call references updated accordingly.
Added or propagated labels for item-related routines such as it_Throw, itColl_BounceOffVictim, and itColl_BounceOffShield.
Added or propagated player/menu labels such as pl_SetSlottype, pl_LoseStock, pl_SetHUDDamage, mnCharSel_CostumeChange, and mnCharSel_CursorThink.

Updated include/functions.h and include/variables.h comments so the symbol knowledge was visible to later C declarations and split work, even though many entries still had unknown prototypes marked with '?'.
Touched 55 files with 2180 added and 2178 deleted lines, indicating a broad relabeling pass rather than substantial new implementation.
smart_moves: The PR propagated labels through all visible assembly callsites instead of only renaming definitions, making xrefs immediately more searchable and reducing future split ambiguity.
It identified table semantics before full C decompilation: naming ft_OnLoad and the special-move/item callback arrays converts anonymous data labels into recognizable per-fighter callback tables.
The labels use subsystem prefixes such as ft, ftAnim, ftData, ef, efLib, efAsync, it, itColl, pl, and mnCharSel, which makes grep-based exploration much more effective.
Renaming common routines like ef_Spawn and efAsync_Spawn is high leverage because they appear throughout many fighter, item, menu, and stage files.
Header comment updates in include/functions.h and include/variables.h kept global symbol inventories in sync with assembly labels, preserving the labels for future C declarations.
decomp_lessons: Before splitting large assembly files, label high-frequency callees and callback tables; this makes candidate boundaries and subsystem ownership easier to infer.

Anonymous data labels that are loaded via lis/addi pairs can often be understood by comparing repeated table usage patterns; here those became fighter callback arrays like ft_OnItemPickup and ft_SpecialN.
A one-for-one rename pass can be valuable even without matching new C: better names reduce cognitive load and make future decompilation tasks more searchable.
When function prototypes are still unknown, updating the header inventory with named placeholder comments is still useful because it records symbol identity without overclaiming types.
Propagating a label to every branch/callsite avoids mixed old/new symbol names, which otherwise makes future grep and xref analysis incomplete.
naming_conventions: Fighter callback/data tables use ft_ plus action/callback name, for example ft_OnLoad, ft_OnDeath, ft_SpecialHiAir, ft_OnItemRelease, and ft_OnKnockbackEnter.

Per-character fighter load callbacks use ft<Character>_OnLoad, for example ftMario_OnLoad, ftFox_OnLoad, ftCFalcon_OnLoad, ftCLink_OnLoad, ftDrMario_OnLoad, ftGameWatch_OnLoad, ftGKoopa_OnLoad, ftZakoBoy_OnLoad, and ftZakoGirl_OnLoad.
Fighter helper routines use more specific ft sub-prefixes such as ftAnim_SetAnimRate, ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim, ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed, and ftData_SetScale.
Effects routines use ef_ for direct effect spawning, efAsync_ for asynchronous spawning, and efLib_ for library-wide operations such as DestroyAll, PauseAll, and ResumeAll.
Item collision helpers use itColl_ prefixes, shown by itColl_BounceOffVictim and itColl_BounceOffShield.
Player and menu routines use pl_ and mnCharSel_ prefixes, shown by pl_SetSlottype, pl_LoseStock, pl_SetHUDDamage, mnCharSel_CostumeChange, and mnCharSel_CursorThink.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed .global labels and the corresponding local label definitions in assembly without altering instruction bytes.
Updated bl operands from anonymous func_* names to semantic labels, for example bl func_8005FDDC became bl ef_Spawn and bl func_80070B88 became bl ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim.
Updated lis/addi address materialization references to renamed data tables, for example ft_OnItemRelease@ha/@l, ft_OnItemCatch@ha/@l, ft_SpecialS@ha/@l, and ft_OnLoad@ha/@l.
Converted data-table .4byte entries from raw function names to semantic per-character callback names in ft_OnLoad.
Kept unknown function prototypes as commented placeholders in include/functions.h, changing only names and not asserting signatures.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or type ft_OnLoad and the other fighter callback tables.
Search for later corrections or confirmations of ftComm_ClampFalllSpeed spelling and semantics.
Find PRs that give prototypes to ef_Spawn and efAsync_Spawn after this label pass.
Search for later splits of asm/melee/ft/code_8006DBF4.s using ftAnim_SetAnimRate, ftAnim_ApplyPartAnim, or ftData_SetScale.
Search for later item collision decompilation around itColl_BounceOffVictim and itColl_BounceOffShield.
